instructions: Inspect several row-units to determine pivot condition. Wiggle row-units from side to side and measure any movement between the arm and pivot point, then rate the pivot condition for this section of the drill.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate each section of your drill by working rank.
Inspect several row-units in each rank to determine pivot condition. Wiggle row-units from side to side and measure any movement between the arm and pivot point, then rate the pivot condition for each rank.
question: How much side-to-side movement is present?
question_multi_rank: Rate the pivot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Less than 1/4-inch
choices.recommended_action: Pivot wear is within an acceptable range. No immediate bushing or hardware replacement is needed.

choices.label: Between 1/4-inch and 1/2-inch slop
choices.recommended_action: Moderate wear is present. Plan to replace pivot bushings and inspect pins before the next seeding season.

choices.label: Greater than 1/2-inch
choices.recommended_action: Excessive movement indicates worn bushings and pins. Replace pivot bushings and hardware before operating.

instructions: Inspect several row-units to determine overall disc conditions. Measure cutting disc diameter, then rate the overall disc condition across the entire machine. Discs should be replaced at or before 17 inches to ensure working condition.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate disc conditions by working rank.
Inspect several row-units in each rank, measure cutting disc diameter, then rate disc condition for each rank. Discs should be replaced at or before 17 inches to ensure working condition.
link_text: We recommend using the Red E Inspection Tool to measure disc diameter.
question: What is the overall disc diameter?
question_multi_rank: Rate the disc diameter for each working rank:

choices.label: Diameter greater than 17.25 inches
choices.recommended_action: Disc diameter is within an acceptable range. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: Diameter is between 17 and 17.25 inches
choices.recommended_action: Discs show early wear. Plan replacement before diameter drops below 17 inches.

choices.label: Diameter less than 17 inches
choices.recommended_action: Replace worn discs measuring less than 17 inches and inspect bearings before operating.

instructions: Inspect several row-units and check for side-to-side movement at the closing wheel pivot. Measure slop between the arm and pivot point, then rate pivot condition for this section of the drill.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate closing wheel pivot condition by working rank.
Inspect several row-units in each rank, check for side-to-side movement at the pivot, then rate pivot condition for each rank.
question: How much side-to-side movement is present at the closing wheel pivot?
question_multi_rank: Rate the closing wheel pivot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: All move up and down, and are tight from side to side
choices.recommended_action: Closing wheel pivot wear is within an acceptable range. No immediate bushing or hardware replacement is needed.

choices.label: Less than 1/4" slop side to side
choices.recommended_action: Moderate closing wheel pivot wear is present. Plan to replace bushings and inspect pins before the next seeding season.

choices.label: Stuck or greater than 1/4" slop
choices.recommended_action: Replace closing wheel pivot hardware before operating.

step_title: Closing Wheel Spring and Arm Inspection
instructions: If the spring is sloppy or broken it won't properly close the furrow, leaving the seed exposed. Inspect closing wheel springs and arms on several row-units. Look for sloppy or broken springs, plastic deformation, and whether springs are on the top adjustment setting.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate closing wheel spring and arm condition by working rank.
If the spring is sloppy or broken it won't properly close the furrow, leaving the seed exposed. Inspect several row-units in each rank and rate spring and arm condition for each rank.
question: What is the overall closing wheel spring and arm condition?
question_multi_rank: Rate the closing wheel spring and arm condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Good working order
choices.recommended_action: Closing wheel springs and arms are in good working order. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: On top adjustment setting - PRETTY SURE THIS CAN BE BETTER
choices.recommended_action: Springs are on the top adjustment setting. Monitor closing performance and adjust as needed before seeding.

choices.label: Broken plastic, plastic deformation, or loose hardware
choices.recommended_action: Replace broken springs or arms showing plastic deformation to restore proper furrow closing.

secondary_choices.label: HD Red E Square Wire WHAT??
Bearing in Arm -WHAT DOES THIS MEAN?
Bearing in CW -WHAT DOES THIS MEAN?

instructions: Inspect closing wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check bearing smoothness, rubber condition, cracks, chips, and uneven wear.
question: What is the overall closing wheel condition?

choices.label: Wheel and bearing in good working condition
choices.recommended_action: Closing wheels and bearings are in good working order. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: Wheel and bearing showing wear
choices.recommended_action: Closing wheel performance may vary. Experiment with setup adjustments and monitor during seeding.

choices.label: Deteriorated wheel or bearing
choices.recommended_action: Replace deteriorated closing wheels and/or bearings before operating.

question_multi_rank: Rate the closing wheel condition for each working rank:
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate this section by working rank.
Inspect closing wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check bearing smoothness, rubber condition, cracks, chips, and uneven wear.

instructions: If the pivot is sloppy or broken it won't properly press the seed into the furrow. Inspect several row-units and check for side-to-side movement at the press wheel pivot. Measure slop between the arm and pivot point, then rate pivot condition for this section of the drill.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate press-wheel-pivot condition by working rank.
If the pivot is sloppy or broken it won't properly press the seed into the furrow. Inspect several row-units in each rank, check for side-to-side movement at the pivot, then rate pivot condition for each rank.
question: How much side-to-side movement is present at the press wheel pivot?
question_multi_rank: Rate the press wheel pivot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: All move up and down, and are tight from side-to-side
choices.recommended_action: Press wheel pivot wear is within an acceptable range. No immediate bushing or hardware replacement is needed.

choices.label: Less than 1/4" slop from side-to-side
choices.recommended_action: Moderate press wheel pivot wear is present. Plan to replace bushings and inspect pins before the next seeding season.

choices.label: Stuck or greater than 1/4" slop from side-to-side
choices.recommended_action: Replace press wheel pivot hardware before operating.

instructions: If the spring is sloppy or broken it won't properly press the seeds into the furrow. Inspect press wheel springs and arms on several row-units. Look for sloppy or broken springs, plastic deformation, and whether springs are on the top adjustment setting.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate press wheel spring and arm condition by working rank.
If the spring is sloppy or broken it won't properly press the seeds into the furrow. Inspect several row-units in each rank and rate spring and arm condition for each rank.
question: What is the overall press wheel spring and arm condition?
question_multi_rank: Rate the press wheel spring and arm condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Good working order
choices.recommended_action: Press wheel springs and arms are in good working order. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: On top adjustment setting (can we do better?)
choices.recommended_action: Springs are on the top adjustment setting. Monitor press wheel performance and adjust as needed before seeding.

choices.label: Broken or plastic deformation (can we do better?)
choices.recommended_action: Replace broken springs or arms showing plastic deformation to restore proper seed press into the furrow.

instructions: Inspect press wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check bearing smoothness, rubber or cast condition, cracks, and uneven wear.
question: What is the overall press wheel condition?

choices.label: Wheel and bearing in good working condition
choices.recommended_action: Press wheels and bearings are in good working order. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: Wheel and bearing showing wear
choices.recommended_action: Some press wheels show early wear. Schedule replacement and inspect pivots and bearings.

choices.label: Deteriorated wheel or bearing
choices.recommended_action: Replace deteriorated press wheels and/or bearings before operating.

question_multi_rank: Rate the press wheel condition for each working rank:
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate this section by working rank.
Inspect press wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check bearing smoothness, rubber or cast condition, cracks, and uneven wear.

instructions: Inspect disc hubs on several row-units in each rank. Check for bearing play, seal condition, wobble, and heat discoloration.
question: What is the overall disc hub condition?

choices.label: All spin freely with no catching
choices.recommended_action: Disc hubs spin freely with no catching. No immediate hub or bearing replacement is needed.

choices.label: Some discs are tight but spin smoothly
choices.recommended_action: Some disc hubs are tight but still spin smoothly. Monitor during seeding and plan service if catching develops.

choices.label: Discs don't spin or catching is felt
choices.recommended_action: Replace or service disc hubs and bearings that do not spin freely or are catching before operating.

question_multi_rank: Rate the disc hub condition for each working rank:
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate this section by working rank.
Inspect disc hubs on several row-units in each rank. Check for bearing play, seal condition, wobble, and heat discoloration.

instructions: Inspect several row-units and check for movement at the depth adjustment pivot. Measure slop and rate pivot condition for this section of the drill.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate depth adjustment pivot condition by working rank.
Inspect several row-units in each rank, check pivot movement, then rate pivot condition for each rank.
question: How much movement is present at the depth adjustment pivot?
question_multi_rank: Rate the depth adjustment pivot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Adjustment moves freely
choices.recommended_action: Depth adjustment pivots move freely. No immediate bushing or hardware replacement is needed.

choices.label: All move, but some are tighter
choices.recommended_action: Some depth adjustment pivots are tighter than others. Plan to inspect and service before the next seeding season.

choices.label: Many stuck or barely moving
choices.recommended_action: Replace or service depth adjustment pivot hardware that is stuck or barely moving before operating.

step_title: Depth Arm, Cover, and Handle Inspection
instructions: Inspect depth arms, covers, and handles on several row-units in each rank. Look for bent arms, missing covers, damaged handles, and loose hardware.
question: What is the overall depth arm, cover, and handle condition?

choices.label: All parts are tight, and in good working order
choices.recommended_action: Depth arms, covers, and handles are all tight. No immediate repair is needed.

choices.label: Some play is present, but depth will be consistent
choices.recommended_action: Some play is present but depth control should stay consistent. Monitor and plan repairs before seeding season.

choices.label: Lots of movement is present, and parts are in poor working order
choices.recommended_action: Replace or repair depth arms, covers, and handles with excessive movement to maintain proper depth control.

question_multi_rank: Rate the depth arm, cover, and handle condition for each working rank:
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate this section by working rank.
Inspect depth arms, covers, and handles on several row-units in each rank. Look for bent arms, missing covers, damaged handles, and loose hardware.

instructions: Inspect gauge wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check tread condition, sidewall wear, bearing operation, and contact with opener discs.
question: What is the overall gauge wheel condition?

choices.label: Tires are in good shape
choices.recommended_action: Gauge wheel tires are in good shape. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: Some wear is present, tires could be replaced
choices.recommended_action: Some gauge wheel wear is present. Plan replacement of wheels, bearings, or arms as needed.

choices.label: Damaged / cracked / weathered tires, should be replaced
choices.recommended_action: Replace damaged, cracked, or weathered gauge wheels, bearings, or arms to restore proper depth control and disc contact.

question_multi_rank: Rate the gauge wheel condition for each working rank:
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate gauge wheels by working rank.
Inspect gauge wheels on several row-units in each rank. Check tread condition, sidewall wear, bearing operation, and contact with opener discs.

instructions: If the boot can move vertically it will cause erratic seed placement. Inspect several row-units and check for movement at the seed boot pivot. Measure slop and rate pivot condition for this section of the drill.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate seed boot pivot condition by working rank.
If the boot can move vertically it will cause erratic seed placement. Inspect several row-units in each rank, check pivot movement, then rate pivot condition for each rank.
question: How much slop is present at the seed boot pivot?
question_multi_rank: Rate the seed boot pivot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Less than 1/4" slop
choices.recommended_action: Seed boot pivot wear is within an acceptable range. No immediate bushing or hardware replacement is needed.

choices.label: Less than 1/2" slop
choices.recommended_action: Moderate seed boot pivot wear is present. Plan to replace bushings and inspect pins before the next seeding season.

choices.label: Greater than 3/4" slop
choices.recommended_action: Excessive seed boot pivot movement indicates worn bushings and pins. Replace hardware before operating.

instructions: A worn seed boot can throw seed out the side of the furrow instead of placing it where it belongs. Inspect seed boots on several row-units in each rank. Check the delivery surfaces for wear, cracks, and missing material.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate seed boot condition by working rank.
A worn seed boot can throw seed out the side of the furrow instead of placing it where it belongs. Inspect several row-units in each rank and check delivery surfaces for wear, cracks, and missing material.
question: What is the overall seed boot condition?
question_multi_rank: Rate the seed boot condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Like new
choices.recommended_action: Seed boots are in like-new condition. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: Some wear is present, but in okay condition
choices.recommended_action: Seed boots still have some life left but show wear. Plan replacement to maintain accurate seed placement.

choices.label: Worn through
choices.recommended_action: Replace worn-through seed boots before seeding to prevent seed from being ejected out the side of the furrow.

instructions: A worn seed-boot-spring may not hold the boot against the disc or keep it in the furrow. Inspect boot springs on several row-units in each rank. Look for weak, broken, missing, or overstretched springs and loose hardware.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate seed-boot-spring condition by working rank.
A worn seed-boot-spring may not hold the boot against the disc or keep it in the furrow. Inspect several row-units in each rank and look for weak, broken, missing, or overstretched springs and loose hardware.
question: What is the overall seed-boot-spring condition?
question_multi_rank: Rate the seed-boot-spring condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Good condition
choices.recommended_action: Seed-boot-springs are in like-new condition. No replacement is needed.

choices.label: Marginal condition. Replace, if already servicing this part of the row-unit.
choices.recommended_action: Seed-boot-springs are worn but still serviceable. Plan replacement when other row-unit work is scheduled.

choices.label: Replace
choices.recommended_action: Replace worn or broken seed-boot-springs before seeding so the boot stays against the disc and in the furrow.

instructions: Worn seed tabs make it easier for seed to bounce out of the furrow. Inspect seed tabs on several row-units in each rank. Check tab height, how much material is left, and overall wear.
instructions_multi_rank:
Evaluate seed tab condition by working rank.
Worn seed tabs make it easier for seed to bounce out of the furrow. Inspect several row-units in each rank and check tab height, remaining material, and wear.
question: How much material is left on the seed tabs?
question_multi_rank: Rate the seed tab condition for each working rank:

choices.label: 90% or more remaining
choices.recommended_action: Seed tabs have plenty of material left. No immediate replacement is needed.

choices.label: 40% or more remaining
choices.recommended_action: Seed tabs are wearing but still have usable life. Plan replacement before seeding season.

choices.label: Less than 15% remaining
choices.recommended_action: Replace worn seed tabs before seeding to help keep seed in the furrow.

instructions: Inspect hoses on the drill by working rank. Look for cracks, UV damage, and overall wear.
instructions_multi_rank: Inspect hoses on the drill by working rank. Look for cracks, UV damage, and overall wear.
question: What is the overall hose condition?
question_multi_rank: Rate the hose condition for each working rank:

choices.label: Good condition
choices.recommended_action: Drill hoses are in acceptable condition.

choices.label: Okay condition
choices.recommended_action: Some drill hoses show early wear or loose fittings. Plan hose replacement or service before seeding season.

choices.label: Replace
choices.recommended_action: Replace damaged drill hoses and fittings before operating to prevent product loss and downtime.

instructions: Inspect all towers on the drill. Check for cracks, corrosion, and overall wear.
question: How many towers need replacing?

choices.label: Replace
choices.recommended_action: Repair or replace damaged towers with stainless steel replacements.

good_condition_action: Drill towers are in acceptable condition.

question: What is the condition of your blockage system?

choices.label: Good
choices.recommended_action: The blockage system is in good working order.

choices.label: Needs Fixing
choices.recommended_action: The blockage system needs fixing. Plan service before seeding season.

choices.label: Needs Replacing
choices.recommended_action: The blockage system needs replacing before operating.

choices.label: Not Installed
choices.recommended_action: No blockage system is currently installed on this drill.

secondary_question: Are you interested in a blockage system?

tertiary_choices.label: J.Assy (a fully-wireless system that clamps on to your hoses)

instructions: Inspect each component on the tank for wear, damage, or operational issues.
question: Rate each component on this tank.

choices.label: Good shape, minimal rust and swelling
choices.recommended_action: Component is in good shape with minimal rust and swelling.

choices.label: Starting to rust or swell but no air leaks
choices.recommended_action: Component is starting to rust or swell. Plan service before seeding season.

choices.label: Leaking air, major rust or swelling
choices.recommended_action: Repair or replace component before operating.

instructions: Inspect the air cart fans for wear, damage, or operational issues.
question: How many fans need replacing?

choices.label: Replace
choices.recommended_action: Repair or replace damaged air cart fans before operating.

good_condition_action: Air cart fans are in acceptable condition.

choices.recommended_action: Plan to replace the auger before operating.

choices.label: Yes
choices.recommended_action: Plan to replace the conveyor before operating.
